codeReview.summary: The fix resolves the original blocking defect from m7-onboard-sponsor-flow: sponsor-side runtime onboarding is no longer dispatched against self, and the added unit assertions verify the session reaches awaiting_adoption with a non-self target plus a pending Onboard op. The prior UnknownPeer immediate-failure path is therefore addressed.

issues.description: The new multi-device spec still bypasses requester-side package restoration by seeding tab B directly via __iglooTestSeedRuntime instead of consuming the generated bfonboard package. This leaves a coverage gap versus the feature requirement that tab B restore/adopt from the package path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: protocol-flow-worker marks agent-browser as mandatory for multi-device validation, but this worker run used Playwright-only verification and still reported followedProcedure=true with no deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/protocol-flow-worker/SKILL.md states under Required Skills: "agent-browser — mandatory for multi-device validation of any protocol ceremony"; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 2ddf03ce-039e-460a-a657-038ed8f78c01 contains no agent-browser invocation and handoff.skillFeedback shows followedProcedure=true, deviations=[].

summary: PASS: reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit bc7a9ae diff for fix-m7-onboard-self-peer-rejection, plus the original failed-feature handoff (m7-onboard-sponsor-flow). The core self-peer UnknownPeer regression is fixed by non-self dispatch targeting and test coverage, with one non-blocking e2e coverage gap recorded.
